tasks.description: D9 usecase hotfix (baseline commit 7a50d745): modify libs/usecase/src/pre_review_gate.rs so that PreReviewGateInteractor::check and CoverageVerifyInteractor::verify_coverage short-circuit to Passed when task-contract.json is absent (TaskContractNotFound -> return Ok(Passed)). Rename the two affected test functions to the *_yields_passed_via_d9_tolerance pattern (missing_task_contract_yields_passed_via_d9_tolerance, coverage_missing_task_contract_yields_passed_via_d9_tolerance) to make D9 origin traceable. Update the TaskContractNotFound docstring on PreReviewGateError to clarify that both interactors return Passed when the file is absent. Also create libs/usecase/src/pre_review_gate/helpers.rs, extracting 7 pub(super) pure helper free functions (blocked_coverage_outcome, blocked_outcome, build_scope_entries, collect_non_canonical_layer_violations, collect_per_layer_violations, collect_task_key_ri_violations, entry_key_to_contracted_ref) from pre_review_gate.rs to keep the parent module under the 700-line verify-module-size cap. This follows the ref_verify.rs + ref_verify/*.rs submodule split pattern. All content delivered at baseline commit 7a50d745.

D9 Makefile.toml hotfix (baseline commit 7a50d745): switch verify-spec-states-current-local to script_runner = "@shell" and add a TRACK_ID + spec.json existence guard. When spec.json is absent (Phase 0 / pre-Phase-1 tracks), emit [SKIP] and exit 0 so that cargo make ci passes on pre-spec tracks. Restores the absent-spec.json tolerance pattern (ADR 2026-06-03-1241-spec-states-gate-tolerate-missing-spec-artifact; same hot-restore as commit 04dcdaf6) as the third sibling precedent alongside review-gate and spec-states tolerances. Content delivered at baseline commit 7a50d745.

D1 — Create .claude/skills/diagnose/SKILL.md: the /track:diagnose one-shot diagnostic skill definition. The file must document all of the following: (1) Trigger conditions — the two primary trigger axes are (a) bin/sotp task-contract check returns PreReviewGateOutcome::Blocked with a list of entries that failed liveness, and (b) step 6 plan-artifacts reviewer scope surfaces signal 🔴 or structural mismatch findings; the skill may also be invoked manually with any diagnostic text. (2) Context files — before rendering a routing decision, read spec.json, all <layer>-types.json catalogues, impl-plan.json, all <layer>-*-signals.json documents, and any source files cited in the findings. (3) LLM-semantic routing judgment process — traverse the SoT hierarchy (ADR -> spec -> catalogue -> impl) top-down, identify the most upstream phase where the root cause originates, assign routing_target from the 5-class taxonomy: adr (missing architectural decision), spec (ADR decision exists but spec.json failed to capture it), type (spec is correct but <layer>-types.json has architectural defect), impl_plan (types are correct but impl-plan task description is missing or wrong), impl (design chain is correct; the source implementation violates the contract). (4) Output contract — return exactly three top-level fields: routing_target (one of adr/spec/type/impl_plan/impl), reason (non-empty string explaining which signal/finding caused the routing decision), recommended_next_action (non-empty string specifying the concrete next step for the calling orchestrator). Constraints: SKILL.md must NOT cite the ADR by date-slug file path. SKILL.md must NOT include writer-subagent launch instructions (writer dispatch is the calling orchestrator's responsibility). No regex or keyword matching rule tables — routing is LLM-semantic only. Provider selection is documented as 'resolved from capabilities.rollback-diagnoser in .harness/config/agent-profiles.json'.

D8 — Add rollback-diagnoser capability entry to .harness/config/agent-profiles.json. Add a new key rollback-diagnoser inside the capabilities object, with provider and model fields in the same JSON structure as existing capability entries (e.g., spec-designer, type-designer, impl-planner). A defensible default provider (e.g., matching the existing spec-designer / orchestrator provider) should be chosen; consumers can override by editing agent-profiles.json. The /track:diagnose skill resolves its LLM invocation target from capabilities.rollback-diagnoser.provider and capabilities.rollback-diagnoser.model. No other files need modification for this task.

D3 — Modify apps/cli-driver/src/task_contract.rs: add a soft-prompt branch to TaskContractDriver::handle so that when the liveness gate returns PreReviewGateOutcome::Blocked, the stderr output includes advisory text recommending the caller invoke /track:diagnose with the blocked entries as diagnostic input. The public signature of handle is unchanged (same CommandOutcome return type, same TaskContractInput parameter — Chain 3 is already 🔵 on structural shape). The Blocked exit code and violation list rendering are already present; this task adds only the /track:diagnose suggestion text to the stderr output that accompanies a Blocked result. The Check variant of TaskContractInput triggers this path; the Coverage variant is not affected. No new PreToolUse hook, lock file, or bypass mechanism is introduced (CN-06).

summary: Five tasks across three sections. S1 (T001-T002) records D9 hotfix work already committed at baseline 7a50d745: PreReviewGate absent-task-contract tolerance + helpers.rs module split (T001) and Makefile.toml spec-states skip restore (T002). S2 (T003-T004) covers the non-Rust new-artifact work for D1 and D8: SKILL.md for /track:diagnose (T003) and the rollback-diagnoser capability entry in agent-profiles.json (T004). S3 (T005) is the sole remaining Rust behavioral change: soft-prompt addition to TaskContractDriver::handle on Blocked outcome (D3).

sections.title: D9 hotfix — baseline-commit work (done at 7a50d745)
description: T001 implements the D9 usecase changes: PreReviewGateInteractor::check and CoverageVerifyInteractor::verify_coverage now short-circuit to Passed when task-contract.json is absent (TaskContractNotFound -> return Ok(Passed)), making Phase 0 baseline commits gate-pass without a task-contract.json on disk. Two test functions are renamed to the *_yields_passed_via_d9_tolerance pattern to make D9 origin traceable in the test suite. helpers.rs is created alongside pre_review_gate.rs, extracting 7 pub(super) pure helpers to keep the parent module under the 700-line verify-module-size cap.

T002 implements the Makefile.toml half of D9: verify-spec-states-current-local gains a TRACK_ID + spec.json existence guard that emits [SKIP] and exits 0 when spec.json is absent, restoring Phase 0 CI tolerance that was lost after PR #175 introduced the gate without a skip guard.
Both tasks are done at baseline commit 7a50d745 and require no further implementation work.

sections.title: D1 + D8 — New skill definition and capability entry (non-Rust artifacts)
description: T003 authors the /track:diagnose SKILL.md at .claude/skills/diagnose/SKILL.md. The skill is a one-shot LLM-semantic diagnostic: it reads the SoT chain context (spec, catalogues, signals, source), identifies the most upstream phase where the mismatch originates, and returns a 3-field routing decision (routing_target, reason, recommended_next_action). No regex or keyword tables. No writer subagent launch — dispatch is always the calling orchestrator's responsibility. ADR not cited by date-slug path per pre-track-adr-authoring.md constraint.

T004 adds the rollback-diagnoser capability to .harness/config/agent-profiles.json with provider and model fields. The /track:diagnose skill resolves its LLM invocation target from this entry, keeping provider selection out of the skill definition and allowing override without touching SKILL.md.
S2 has no Rust source changes. These tasks have no catalogue entries in task-contract.json.

sections.title: D3 — CLI driver soft prompt for /track:diagnose on Blocked (Rust behavioral change)
description: T005 is the only remaining Rust behavioral change from D1-D8. TaskContractDriver::handle gains a soft-prompt branch: when bin/sotp task-contract check returns Blocked, stderr now includes advisory text suggesting the caller invoke /track:diagnose with the blocked entries as diagnostic input.

The public API signature of handle is unchanged; Chain 3 is already 🔵 on structural shape match. The Blocked exit code and violation list rendering are unchanged; only the soft-prompt advisory text is added per D3.
T005 is attributed to cli_driver:TaskContractDriver (and the co-located cli_driver:TaskContractInput reference entry) in task-contract.json.
